originalTitle: Oil prices fall 3% on report Trump reluctant to restart Iran war
summary: President Donald Trump told aides that he won't resume the war unless U.S. troops are killed, according to The Wall Street Journal.
content:
Oil prices dropped about 3% Thursday, on a report that President Donald Trump is reluctant to resume full-scare war with Iran despite recent clashes.

West Texas Intermediate futures declined 3.1% to close at $93.04 per barrel. Brent futures, the international benchmark, lost 2.8% to settle at $95.03 per barrel.

Trump told his aides that the weekslong ceasefire with Iran is holding despite sporadic clashes, unnamed U.S. officials told The Wall Street Journal . The president said he would consider ending the truce if Iran kills American troops, the officials said.

The White House declined to comment on the report when asked by CNBC. A White House official said while Trump "always prefers a diplomatic solution, he has been clear about the consequences if Iran refuses to make a deal."

The ceasefire appeared on the verge of collapsing earlier this week after Iranian state media said Tehran had cut off talks with the U.S. due to Israel's military campaign in Lebanon. Iran backs Hezbollah militants in Lebanon who have fired missiles at Israel.

Israel and Lebanon agreed on Wednesday to implement a ceasefire, which could help advance talks between the U.S. and Iran. But it is unclear whether a Lebanon ceasefire would hold. Hezbollah operates independently from the government in Beirut.

Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu told CNBC on Wednesday that "we have to disarm Hezbollah and we have to demilitarize Lebanon."

Trump is facing growing opposition to the war in the Republican-led Congress. The House of Representatives passed a resolution Wednesday that called on Trump to withdraw U.S. forces or seek congressional approval to continue the conflict. The resolution still has to pass the Senate and would almost certainly be vetoed by Trump.
chineseTitle: 特朗普不愿重启伊朗战争，油价下跌 3%
chineseSummary: 据《华尔街日报》报道，唐纳德·特朗普总统告诉助手，除非美军被杀，否则他不会恢复战争。
chineseContent:
据报道，尽管最近发生了冲突，唐纳德·特朗普总统仍不愿与伊朗恢复全面战争，周四油价下跌约 3%。

西德克萨斯中质原油期货下跌 3.1%，收于每桶 93.04 美元。国际基准布伦特原油期货下跌 2.8%，收于每桶 95.03 美元。

不愿透露姓名的美国官员告诉《华尔街日报》，特朗普告诉他的助手，尽管发生了零星冲突，但与伊朗长达数周的停火仍在继续。官员们表示，总统表示，如果伊朗杀死美军，他将考虑结束休战。

当 CNBC 询问时，白宫拒绝对该报道发表评论。一名白宫官员表示，虽然特朗普“总是倾向于通过外交解决方案，但他很清楚如果伊朗拒绝达成协议的后果”。

本周早些时候，伊朗官方媒体称，由于以色列在黎巴嫩的军事行动，德黑兰中断了与美国的谈判，停火协议似乎濒临崩溃。伊朗支持黎巴嫩真主党武装分子向以色列发射导弹。

以色列和黎巴嫩周三同意实施停火，这可能有助于推进美国和伊朗之间的谈判。但尚不清楚黎巴嫩停火能否持续。真主党独立于贝鲁特政府运作。

以色列总理本杰明·内塔尼亚胡周三对 CNBC 表示，“我们必须解除真主党的武装，我们必须使黎巴嫩非军事化。”

特朗普在共和党领导的国会中面临着越来越多的反对战争的声音。众议院周三通过一项决议，呼吁特朗普撤军或寻求国会批准继续冲突。该决议仍需在参议院获得通过，而且几乎肯定会被特朗普否决。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
